Description: You enter a room of doom.
Prompt: Do you face your fears and commit to a leap of faith?

YesChoice.Flavor: The pit was a facade, and you stubbed your toe on it.

Effects.Flavor: You die of embarrassment.

NoChoice.Prompt: I'm too young to die!
NoChoice.Flavor: A pigman pushes you in and you feel air whipping past your face.

Effects.Flavor: You die of falling.

Description: A room filled with the smell of fresh bread. A loaf sits on a pedestal, golden and warm.
Prompt: Do you eat the bread?

YesChoice.Prompt: I have not eaten in days.
YesChoice.Flavor: It tastes exactly like your grandmother's kitchen. A single tear rolls down your cheek.

Effects.Flavor: You gain +2 nostalgia and forget why you came here.

NoChoice.Prompt: Bread on a pedestal is never just bread.
NoChoice.Flavor: Smart. The bread slowly opens one eye and glares at you as you pass.

Effects.Flavor: You earn the Bread's grudging respect.

Description: A long corridor lined with portraits. Every painting's eyes follow you. At the end, a mirror shows a reflection that is one step behind.
Prompt: Do you wave at your reflection?

YesChoice.Flavor: It waves back. Then it waves again. And again. It won't stop waving.

Effects.Flavor: You are now haunted by enthusiasm.

NoChoice.Prompt: I refuse to acknowledge this.
NoChoice.Flavor: Your reflection sighs deeply, folds its arms, and sits down on the floor in protest.

Effects.Flavor: Your self-image takes a hit.

Description: A cozy library. An armchair sits beside a roaring fire. A cat is asleep on the chair.
Prompt: Do you move the cat?

YesChoice.Flavor: The cat opens one eye. You feel a chill that has nothing to do with temperature.

Effects.Flavor: You are cursed with perpetual discomfort in all future seating.

NoChoice.Prompt: The cat was here first.
NoChoice.Flavor: You sit on the cold floor. The cat purrs. Something about this feels right.

Effects.Flavor: The cat will remember this. That might be good.

Description: A damp cave. A troll sits under a stone bridge, eating what appears to be a very small sandwich.
Prompt: Do you ask the troll for a bite of the sandwich?

YesChoice.Flavor: The troll stares at you for a long, horrible moment, then hands you half.

Effects.Flavor: It was a good sandwich. You and the troll are now friends.

NoChoice.Prompt: I will not beg a troll for food.
NoChoice.Flavor: You attempt to sneak past. The troll doesn't notice you. It is very absorbed in its sandwich.

Effects.Flavor: You are fine. You are also a little hungry.

Description: A vast ballroom. A single chandelier flickers overhead. Dozens of skeletons stand frozen mid-waltz.
Prompt: Do you play the piano in the corner?

YesChoice.Flavor: The skeletons resume their waltz instantly. They are better dancers than you expected.

Effects.Flavor: You have accidentally hosted a very successful party.

NoChoice.Prompt: I am not a music box for the undead.
NoChoice.Flavor: Wise. The piano has too many keys and not enough of them are the right ones.

Effects.Flavor: The skeletons remain still. They look disappointed.

Description: A stone room with a single lever on the wall. A sign above it reads: DO NOT PULL.
Prompt: Do you pull the lever?

YesChoice.Prompt: Signs like this are basically invitations.
YesChoice.Flavor: A distant rumbling. Then silence. Then a cake descends from the ceiling on a rope.

Effects.Flavor: The cake is real. No one is more surprised than you.

NoChoice.Prompt: I respect signage.
NoChoice.Flavor: You leave the lever alone. The sign seems to nod approvingly. Signs cannot nod.

Effects.Flavor: Nothing happens. You feel strangely good about this.

Description: A fog-filled room. Something large breathes in the darkness ahead. It sounds asleep.
Prompt: Do you try to sneak past?

YesChoice.Flavor: You tiptoe successfully. Halfway through, you sneeze. The breathing pauses. Then resumes.

Effects.Flavor: You survive on luck alone, as is tradition.

NoChoice.Prompt: I announce myself like a person of honor.
NoChoice.Flavor: You clear your throat. The breathing stops. Two enormous eyes open in the dark.

Effects.Flavor: It turns out the creature just wanted someone to talk to. This takes a while.

Description: A pristine white room. In the center, a button. The button is red. The button says DEFINITELY DO NOT PRESS.
Prompt: Do you press the button?

YesChoice.Flavor: A brief jingle plays. A flag pops up that says 'OOPS.'

Effects.Flavor: Something somewhere has gone wrong. You will never know what.

NoChoice.Prompt: I have read enough to know better.
NoChoice.Flavor: You walk past. The button dims slightly, as though sulking.

Effects.Flavor: You have wounded the button's pride. It will recover.

Description: A room knee-deep in gold coins. A dragon is asleep atop the pile, wearing a small nightcap.
Prompt: Do you take a coin?

YesChoice.Flavor: The dragon's nostril twitches. Its eyes remain closed. It whispers, without waking: 'I noticed.'

Effects.Flavor: You keep the coin. You also keep the anxiety.

NoChoice.Prompt: I value my life over currency.
NoChoice.Flavor: You back out slowly. The dragon's tail curls into what might be a thumbs-up.

Effects.Flavor: The dragon respects your restraint. This is worth more than a coin.

Description: A room filled entirely with doors. Every door is labeled 'EXIT' except one, which is labeled 'NOT THE EXIT.'
Prompt: Do you try the NOT THE EXIT door?

YesChoice.Prompt: Reverse psychology is a two-way street.
YesChoice.Flavor: It is the exit. You were right to be suspicious of signs.

Effects.Flavor: You escape. Your faith in irony is rewarded.

NoChoice.Prompt: I will take the logical door.
NoChoice.Flavor: All the EXIT doors lead to a second room filled entirely with doors.

Effects.Flavor: You are now in door purgatory.

Description: A greenhouse room, warm and humid. Beautiful flowers bloom in every color. A small sign reads: DO NOT SMELL THE BLACK ONES.
Prompt: Do you smell the black flowers?

YesChoice.Prompt: Beauty is meant to be experienced.
YesChoice.Flavor: The scent is indescribable — like lightning, old books, and regret.

Effects.Flavor: You briefly understand the universe. Then you forget all of it.

NoChoice.Prompt: I have made my peace with curiosity.
NoChoice.Flavor: You admire them from a distance. They seem to bloom more brightly for the attention.

Effects.Flavor: The black flowers remember you fondly.

Description: A sparse room. A wizard stands in the center, holding a hat. He offers to guess your weight.
Prompt: Do you let him guess?

YesChoice.Prompt: Show me what you've got, wizard.
YesChoice.Flavor: He guesses exactly. To the ounce. He pumps his fist and whispers 'yes.'

Effects.Flavor: He needed this win. Something about the encounter feels oddly wholesome.

NoChoice.Prompt: A wizard's games are never just games.
NoChoice.Flavor: He deflates visibly. He hasn't gotten one right all century.

Effects.Flavor: You feel a little guilty. He was so hopeful.

Description: A small room with a trapdoor in the floor. Muffled laughter drifts up from below.
Prompt: Do you open the trapdoor?

YesChoice.Prompt: I want to know what's funny.
YesChoice.Flavor: A goblin looks up at you mid-giggle and screams. You also scream. The goblin screams again.

Effects.Flavor: Mutual screaming is a form of bonding, apparently.

NoChoice.Prompt: Whatever is laughing down there can stay down there.
NoChoice.Flavor: The laughter continues. It gets louder. Then stops abruptly. The silence is worse.

Effects.Flavor: You will never know. This will bother you.

Description: An icy cavern. A frozen knight stands at attention, encased in a block of ice. His expression is deeply apologetic.
Prompt: Do you thaw the knight?

YesChoice.Prompt: No one should stand in ice for eternity.
YesChoice.Flavor: He thaws instantly. He apologizes. He apologizes again. He is still apologizing.

Effects.Flavor: You have freed a very sorry knight. He follows you now.

NoChoice.Prompt: He looks frozen for a reason.
NoChoice.Flavor: You leave him as is. His apologetic expression seems to intensify.

Effects.Flavor: Whatever he did, he has a very long time to feel bad about it.

Description: A cluttered workshop. Tools hang on every wall. In the corner, a small automaton winds itself up and looks at you expectantly.
Prompt: Do you give the automaton a task?

YesChoice.Flavor: It whirrs away and returns with a single sock. It looks very proud.

Effects.Flavor: You have one sock. It's actually a good sock.

NoChoice.Prompt: I don't trust clockwork things.
NoChoice.Flavor: It winds down slowly, still watching you, hope fading from its tiny glass eyes.

Effects.Flavor: You feel terrible about this for the rest of the dungeon.

Description: A room that smells of ozone. Lightning arcs between two metal pillars. Scratched into the floor: STAND BETWEEN THE PILLARS FOR POWER.
Prompt: Do you stand between the pillars?

YesChoice.Flavor: The lightning passes through you. Every hair on your body stands at attention. You feel tremendous.

Effects.Flavor: You gain the ability to slightly shock people during handshakes. You cannot turn this off.

NoChoice.Prompt: I believe in electricity and also in not touching it.
NoChoice.Flavor: You walk around the pillars. A faint voice from the floor inscription says 'reasonable.'

Effects.Flavor: You survive. Your hair looks nice.

Description: An ornate room with a single throne. A crown rests on the throne. No one is around.
Prompt: Do you put on the crown?

YesChoice.Flavor: It fits perfectly. Somewhere, a fanfare plays briefly, then cuts out like someone forgot to finish it.

Effects.Flavor: You are technically royalty now. No one enforces this.

NoChoice.Prompt: Unclaimed crowns are never unclaimed.
NoChoice.Flavor: You sit on the throne without the crown. It is extremely comfortable. You lose six minutes.

Effects.Flavor: You are rested but uncrowded. Both are valid outcomes.

Description: A rickety room that seems to be the inside of a giant stomach. The walls pulse gently.
Prompt: Do you ask to be digested?

YesChoice.Flavor: The walls seem to consider this. A rumbling that sounds almost like 'alright then.'

Effects.Flavor: You are digested. It is surprisingly peaceful. You emerge somewhere else.

NoChoice.Prompt: I am not food.
NoChoice.Flavor: You assert your non-food status firmly. The walls pulse in what feels like a shrug.

Effects.Flavor: A door appears. The stomach respected your boundaries.

Description: A darkened room. A fortune teller sits behind a crystal ball. She says she already knows what you will choose.
Prompt: Do you ask her what you will choose?

YesChoice.Flavor: She says 'yes.' You realize that was the answer to your question.

Effects.Flavor: You are trapped in a self-fulfilling prophecy of mild inconvenience.

NoChoice.Prompt: I make my own choices.
NoChoice.Flavor: She nods slowly. 'That's what they all say,' she says. 'Right before they ask.'

Effects.Flavor: You leave immediately. She calls after you: 'See you next time.'

Description: A swamp room indoors, somehow. A frog on a lily pad stares at you with ancient, knowing eyes.
Prompt: Do you kiss the frog?

YesChoice.Flavor: The frog becomes a prince. He is very confused and underdressed for a dungeon.

Effects.Flavor: You have a prince now. He is more trouble than the frog was.

NoChoice.Prompt: I do not kiss strangers, regardless of species.
NoChoice.Flavor: The frog blinks. It seems relieved. It returns to staring at the middle distance.

Effects.Flavor: The frog has already forgotten you. This is fine.

Description: A room at a crossroads. A merchant sells one item: a mystery box. He assures you it is worth it.
Prompt: Do you buy the mystery box?

YesChoice.Flavor: You open it. Inside is a slightly smaller mystery box.

Effects.Flavor: Inside that one is a note that says 'nice try.' You keep both boxes.

NoChoice.Prompt: Mystery is another word for disappointment.
NoChoice.Flavor: The merchant sighs and opens it himself. It contains exactly what you would have wanted.

Effects.Flavor: He keeps it. He looks at you like you deserve this.

Description: A narrow room. The ceiling is slowly descending. You have time, but not a lot of it.
Prompt: Do you try to hold up the ceiling?

YesChoice.Flavor: It stops. You stand there straining. Nothing else happens. It just... stops.

Effects.Flavor: Your arms hurt. The ceiling stays put. You may have broken the trap.

NoChoice.Prompt: I will find another way.
NoChoice.Flavor: You sprint for the far door. It opens. The ceiling freezes one inch from the floor behind you.

Effects.Flavor: You escape. You also learned you're faster than you thought.

Description: A serene room with a koi pond in the center. The fish are large, orange, and watching you with unsettling focus.
Prompt: Do you feed the fish?

YesChoice.Flavor: You toss in a crumb. The fish converge instantly. The water churns. Silence returns. The fish resume their positions.

Effects.Flavor: The pond is suspiciously still. You did not ask what those fish eat.

NoChoice.Prompt: Something about those fish is wrong.
NoChoice.Flavor: You back away slowly. One fish mouths what looks like the word 'please.'

Effects.Flavor: You leave. You still think about those fish sometimes.

Description: A dimly lit tavern room, frozen mid-brawl. Chairs hang in the air, mugs suspended mid-spill. The moment you enter, everything resumes.
Prompt: Do you join the brawl?

YesChoice.Prompt: I never needed a reason to fight.
YesChoice.Flavor: You wade in swinging. Several combatants redirect their attention to you.

Effects.Flavor: You have made enemies the old-fashioned way: immediately.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Belligerent Barfly
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: He goes down clutching his mug, muttering something about his tab.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: He lands one magnificent haymaker and you see several extra moons.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Tavern Bouncer
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: He collapses slowly, like a proud building being demolished.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: He grabs you by the collar and introduces you to the floor personally.

NoChoice.Prompt: I'll watch from a safe corner.
NoChoice.Flavor: You find a stool near the wall. A stray chair clips your ear anyway.

Effects.Flavor: Neutrality has its limits in a brawl.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Misdirected Drunk
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: He passes out before hitting the ground. Technically a win.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: He falls on you completely by accident. He is very heavy.

Description: A graveyard room. Headstones jut from the stone floor. The epitaphs all read variations of 'Should have run.'
Prompt: Do you pay your respects and linger?

YesChoice.Flavor: You bow your head. The ground appreciates this. Then it stops appreciating and starts trembling.

Effects.Flavor: Respect, it turns out, is an invitation down here.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Restless Skeleton
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: It collapses into a neat pile of bones, which somehow look more at peace than before.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: It rattles you to death with surprising enthusiasm for something with no muscles.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Grave Warden
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: He slumps against a headstone. His own epitaph writes itself.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: He buries you. In his defense, that is literally his job.

NoChoice.Prompt: I move through graveyards briskly.
NoChoice.Flavor: You speed-walk through, eyes forward. Something claws at your ankle and misses.

Effects.Flavor: Almost. You keep moving.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Ankle-Grabbing Ghoul
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: It dissolves into a wisp, muttering that it almost had you.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: It drags you under with a grip that defies its wispy form.

Description: A throne room occupied by a very small king sitting in a very large chair. He points at you and declares you a threat to the realm.
Prompt: Do you dispute the charge?

YesChoice.Flavor: He slams his tiny fist on the armrest and calls for his guards. The guards are not small.

Effects.Flavor: Due process has failed you.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Royal Guard
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: He salutes as he falls, loyal to the end.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: He runs you through with the formal efficiency of someone who has done this before.

PossibleEnemies.Name: The Tiny King
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: He slides off the throne dramatically. His crown lands on you. It fits.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: Against all odds, he defeats you personally. He celebrates for a long time.

NoChoice.Prompt: I plead guilty to whatever he needs.
NoChoice.Flavor: He squints. He didn't expect that. He confers with his armrest. He lets you go with a warning.

Effects.Flavor: Diplomacy: occasionally just agreeing with small royalty.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Suspicious Court Jester
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: He cartwheels one last time and doesn't come back up.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: He jingles you to death. No one believed this was possible, including him.

Description: A sweltering forge room. Weapons line the walls — all of them unfinished. A massive smith works at the anvil, back turned.
Prompt: Do you take one of the unfinished weapons from the wall?

YesChoice.Prompt: Half a sword is better than none.
YesChoice.Flavor: The smith's hammer stops mid-swing. He turns around slowly. His eye twitches.

Effects.Flavor: You have disturbed his system.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Furious Blacksmith
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: He crashes to the floor like a dropped anvil, which ironically he is standing next to.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: He uses the unfinished blade on you. He finishes it on the way out.

NoChoice.Prompt: I respect craftsmanship and personal space.
NoChoice.Flavor: You watch him work. He is extraordinary. He hands you something small without looking up.

Effects.Flavor: A finished throwing knife. He nods once. You are dismissed.

Description: A flooded room, knee-deep in black water. Something large circles beneath the surface, just visible.
Prompt: Do you wade through?

YesChoice.Prompt: Standing water has never stopped me.
YesChoice.Flavor: The circling thing notices the ripples your legs make. It finds this interesting.

Effects.Flavor: You have advertised your location to whatever this is.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Dungeon Lurker
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: It surfaces one last time, blinks at you slowly, and sinks.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: It pulls you under with a grip like cold iron. The water goes still.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Pale Eel
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: It sparks once and goes dark, drifting to the surface belly-up.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: It shocks you into the water. You float there briefly before the rest happens.

NoChoice.Prompt: I will find another route or wait it out.
NoChoice.Flavor: You wait. The thing circles. Eventually it loses interest and drifts to the far end. You cross quickly.

Effects.Flavor: Patience rewarded. Your boots are still ruined.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Pale Eel
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: It sparks once and goes dark, drifting to the surface belly-up.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: It shocks you into the water. You float there briefly before the rest happens.

Description: A narrow canyon-like room. Eyes glow from crevices in both walls. Many eyes. Blinking at different intervals.
Prompt: Do you make yourself look bigger and shout?

YesChoice.Flavor: Some of the eyes retreat. Three pairs do not. They were never going to.

Effects.Flavor: Partial success. You deal with the remainder.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Cave Stalker
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: It drops from the wall and lies still, eyes finally closed.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: It was behind you the entire time. It was very patient.

NoChoice.Prompt: Shouting in a cave seems inadvisable.
NoChoice.Flavor: You creep through in silence. The eyes track you the whole way. At the end, something drops from the ceiling.

Effects.Flavor: Silence did not help as much as hoped.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Cave Stalker
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: It drops from the wall and lies still, eyes finally closed.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: It was behind you the entire time. It was very patient.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Ceiling Clinger
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: It peels off and falls with a wet finality.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: Falling from the ceiling turns out to be a highly effective attack.

Description: A courtyard room open to a false sky. A scarecrow stands in the center, arms out. It is not stuffed with straw.
Prompt: Do you investigate the scarecrow?

YesChoice.Prompt: Something is wrong with it and I need to know what.
YesChoice.Flavor: Up close, you see it breathing. Slowly. Then less slowly.

Effects.Flavor: You have woken something up by looking at it too hard.

PossibleEnemies.Name: The Scarecrow
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: It returns to its post and goes still. The breathing stops. You're not sure it was ever alive.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: It wraps its arms around you with the slow inevitability of something that has all the time in the world.

NoChoice.Prompt: Scarecrows are not my problem.
NoChoice.Flavor: You give it a wide berth. Its head rotates to follow you. Just its head.

Effects.Flavor: You leave faster than intended.

PossibleEnemies.Name: The Scarecrow
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: It returns to its post and goes still. The breathing stops. You're not sure it was ever alive.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: It wraps its arms around you with the slow inevitability of something that has all the time in the world.

Description: A mess hall. Goblin soldiers eat noisily at long tables, unaware of you. The exit is on the far side.
Prompt: Do you attempt to walk through disguised as one of them?

YesChoice.Prompt: Confidence and a slouch will carry me.
YesChoice.Flavor: It works for twelve incredible seconds. Then someone asks you to pass the slop. You pass it wrong.

Effects.Flavor: There is a correct way to pass goblin slop. You did not do it.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Goblin Soldier
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: He faceplants into his bowl. His comrades pause, then keep eating.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: He stabs you with a fork. It is somehow very effective.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Goblin Sergeant
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: He bellows an order no one follows and then falls over.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: He issues a formal order for your death and personally carries it out.

NoChoice.Prompt: I will cause a distraction instead.
NoChoice.Flavor: You hurl something through the far window. The goblins stampede toward it. You walk through calmly.

Effects.Flavor: Flawless. Except one goblin didn't go. He stayed. He's looking at you.

PossibleEnemies.Name: Suspicious Goblin
PossibleEnemies.DeathFlavor: He points at you accusingly one final time and then slumps.
PossibleEnemies.KilledByFlavor: He was right to be suspicious. He is very smug about this until you die.
